evidence_quotes.quote: The two most salient examples are computer games and IOT connected devices.
Both of these categories have real-world examples of functionality being remotely disabled.
For games, they should not be removed from peoples account nor be rendered inaccessible due to the publishers lack of interest.

paragraphs.text: I am glad of this act which aims to protect consumer rights and reign in the predatory practices which companies are all too comfortable with. As our lives become more connected, we have come to own more items (digital or physical) which can have their access revoked after having purchased them. The two most salient examples are computer games and IOT connected devices. Both of these categories have real-world examples of functionality being remotely disabled. Devices should have a bare minimum functionality required without a connection to the internet, as for games, they should not be removed from peoples account nor be rendered inaccessible due to the publishers lack of interest. For the latter issue the stop destroying videogames initiative is a relevant issue. I urge you to consider these issues for the Digital Fairness Act.
